texto: No matter how much exercise or energy healing I do how many soothing restorative walks I take through the woods, how many heads of raw kale I juice or don't...there is no inoculation against illness. No guarantees, no way to wrestle life under control. There are merely best attempts. But surrendering the false hope that there is a single way to make everything Just So might just be the best strategy of all.
explicacao: Citação de Gillian Deacon sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “No matter how much exercise or energy healing I do how many soothing restorative walks I take thr…” articula reflexao, healthy-living, illness-and-hope em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e illness-and-hope.

frase_original: No matter how much exercise or energy healing I do how many soothing restorative walks I take through the woods, how many heads of raw kale I juice or don't...there is no inoculation against illness. No guarantees, no way to wrestle life under control. There are merely best attempts. texto: There is no social stigma attached to the frenzy, no peer motivation to slow us down. Rather it is the opposite; busy is popular currency, traded among members of modern society like a precious commodity. Busy is the silkiest cloth at the emporium, the most well-travelled spice. Living with a full schedule speedily typed into a pinging, vibrating device is a highly valued state of being. And, as with any addiction, it becomes self-perpetuating. We feel a rush from being in a rush; we take pride in the breakneck pace at which we travel through our days.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Gillian Deacon: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “There is no social stigma attached to the frenzy, no peer motivation to slow us down. Rather it i…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, busyness, life-wisdom pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e life-wisdom.
